frozen_components: In-Context Prompt Generator LLM (GPT-3.5, GPT-4, or Llama-2)
Intent Predictor LLM (GPT-3.5, GPT-4, or Llama-2)
SentenceBERT embedder (for few-shot and intent sampling)
architecture.summary: IntentGPT is a training-free system for few-shot intent discovery that chains two frozen LLMs: an In-Context Prompt Generator (ICPG) that creates a task-specific prompt from labeled training examples, and an Intent Predictor that uses the generated prompt plus semantically-sampled few-shot examples and a dynamically-updated list of known/discovered intents to classify user utterances and discover new intents during inference. Known Intent Feedback loops discovered intents back into the prompt for online learning without parameter updates.

biblio.title: IntentGPT: Few-shot Intent Discovery with Large Language Models

claims.statement: IntentGPT-4 in the 50-shot setting outperforms prior methods in both unsupervised and semi-supervised scenarios on CLINC, achieving ACC of 88.76
IntentGPT-4 in the 50-shot setting outperforms prior methods on BANKING, achieving ACC of 77.21 vs. prior best
IntentGPT-4 achieves ARI of 84.76 on CLINC in the 50-shot setting
IntentGPT-4 achieves ARI of 66.66 on BANKING in the 50-shot setting
IntentGPT even in the 0-shot unsupervised setting outperforms all previous unsupervised methods
Known Intent Feedback (KIF) is essential: without it, the number of discovered intents becomes uncontrolled, indicating the model cannot reuse known intents when not explicitly aware of them
Semantic Known Intent Feedback Sampling (SKIF) does not result in significant gains; optimal performance includes all known intents in the prompt

Strong restriction on context length of LLM; computational and monetary costs increase with context; privacy and security concerns from using external LLM APIs like GPT-4; Llama-2 offers cost-effective local alternative but may have bias concerns
